display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Recent SQL-endpoint
RLS/OLS or unsupported feature,
OR exceeded SKU guardrails?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
FORCED FALLBACK to DirectQuery
SQL OLS/RLS forces it — move RLS to ONE canonical plane

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
FALLBACK to DirectQuery
reduce model size / avoid the feature, or scale SKU

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Fallback was disabled in the model
re-enable, or fix the guardrail/feature cause

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
UNFRAMED / unprocessed table
re-frame after the Delta write; no DQ fallback exists

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
OneLake-security misconfig
RLS/CLS role yields EMPTY, not an error — fix the data-access role

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Volume past Direct Lake limits
or column not V-Ordered?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Re-shape gold: V-Order + framing;
on-OneLake does NOT fall back — it errors past limits

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Trace the query;
confirm framing + role + capacity before rebuilding
